title: Cyclosorus prolixus
taxonomy: Cyclosorus prolixus is classified under the phylum Pteridophyta, which includes ferns and other vascular cryptogams. It belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae, a group of ferns that are native to tropical and subtropical regions. This species is further categorized as a member of the genus Cyclosorus, which is a group of deciduous ferns. Cyclosorus prolixus is also known to be a synonym of the species Christella prolixa. However, the classification may vary depending on the source and recent taxonomic revisions.

intro: Cyclosorus prolixus, also known as the rattan fern, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Thelypteridaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its numerous health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, which can help to alleviate various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and digestive problems. The bioactive constituents of Cyclosorus prolixus include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. In herbal medicine, the plant is commonly prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional Chinese medicine to treat a range of health conditions, including fever, cough, and sore throat.
